fix(engine): critical signal pipeline bugs — conviction scale, NameErrors, BTC data routing

- config.py: MIN_CONVICTION_FOR_DEPLOY 0.60→60 (was never filtering — 45<0.60 is always False)
- main.py: add brain_id="MultiTF-HMM" + _is_reversal_tier2=False in multi-TF path (NameError on every _analyze_coin call)
- main.py: fix KeyError — brain_cfg['label'] on empty dict → hardcoded "MultiTF-HMM" string
- main.py: BTC macro brain only predicts on BTC's own 1D data (was feeding alt-coin daily features to BTC brain)
- main.py: _bcast FILTERED_DUPLICATE now passes confidence (0-1.0) not conviction (0-100) to avoid 7500% log output
- main.py: fix conviction filter log format (removed ×100 multipliers, fixed :.0% → :.0f)
- engine_api.py: /api/set-config no longer crashes with AttributeError on removed STRATEGY_PROFILES/BRAIN_PROFILES

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove stale project/ shadow directory and add .gitignore

The initial commit was made from the parent IGA Milk Order/ directory,
which caused all source files to be tracked with a project/ prefix
(e.g. project/src/pwa/...) alongside the real files at src/pwa/...
This created a 53-file stale shadow directory (project/project/ on disk).

Fixes:
- git rm -r project/ removes all 53 duplicate stale-prefixed files
- .gitignore now blocks project/, node_modules/, dist/, Lactalis Invoices/
  so the shadow directory can never reappear
- Parent repo IGA Milk Order/.git remote removed separately so it
  cannot accidentally push to JARVIS and conflict with this repo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Rename artifacts field to work_products (ISO 15288 alignment)

Tested: PydanticAI mech agent → CLIProxyAPI → Claude OAuth successfully

structured engineering analysis.

commit.message:
fix: remove broken checksum verification from CACHE/LARGE_DATA modes

Buffer checksum approach produces false positives because the number of
FMA passes per batch is non-deterministic (timing-based). The checksum
changes unpredictably between batches, causing every batch to report
an error. NaN/Inf checks are retained for these modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: MSVC Lucas-Lehmer supports p up to 60 via _umul128/_udiv128

The p > 30 limit was too conservative, causing lucas_lehmer(31) to
incorrectly return false. Since _umul128/_udiv128 handle full 128-bit
arithmetic, p up to 60 works correctly (matching the GCC __uint128_t path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ux): v3.7.61 — 더블클릭 2개 인스턴스 완전 수정 + 기동 속도 개선

[버그 1: 근본 원인] 소켓 락이 ensure_postgres_running() 이후에 있어 타이밍 레이스 발생
  - EXE 더블클릭 → postgres 초기화(수 초) 동안 락 미확보 상태
  - 두 번째 더블클릭이 이 사이에 발생하면 둘 다 소켓 bind 성공 → 2개 실행
  - 수정: 소켓 락 획득을 __main__ 진입 직후 첫 번째 동작으로 이동 (ensure_postgres 이전)

[버그 2: 기동 속도] 스플래시 폴링 간격 0.5초 → 0.1초로 단축
  - 서버는 소켓 bind 완료 후 즉시 응답 가능 상태
  - 이전: 최대 5초(10회×0.5초) 대기 / 수정: 최대 3초(30회×0.1초)로 응답성 향상

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: HNSW rebuild debounce, read-lock search, parking_lot::Mutex

- HNSW search uses read lock first, only upgrades to write lock when
  rebuild is needed — eliminates write contention on concurrent searches
- Rebuild debounce: 5-second cooldown prevents thrashing under burst
  search patterns (configurable via rebuild_cooldown_secs)
- Extract search_inner_readonly helper for lock-free search path
- SqliteStorage: switch from std::sync::Mutex to parking_lot::Mutex
  (no poisoning, faster lock/unlock for short critical sections)
- Cortex retrieval_cache: same parking_lot::Mutex upgrade

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SSE verify must use nofma expected (separate mul+add, not std::fma)

SSE intrinsics use _mm_mul_pd + _mm_add_pd (two rounding steps) while
compute_scalar_expected uses std::fma (one rounding step). Over 10K
iterations, this difference accumulates to detectable bit-level errors.

Standardize device selection — Zoom/Teams-style inline pickers

Replace SelectSourcePopup modal with inline DevicePicker dropdowns
matching the UX pattern of Zoom, Teams, and Meet:

- Auto-connect camera + mic on Hub mount using system defaults
  (prefers last-used camera as ideal hint via profile API)

  open a list, click a device to switch instantly, no Connect button
- Device labels stripped of hardware ID suffixes for cleaner display
- Permission errors shown in the video area with actionable message
  and Try Again button instead of buried in a popup
- Audio output section removed (OS manages speaker routing)
- SplashScreen login simplified — HubScreen owns all device setup
- SelectSourcePopup deleted; voicePaused still used by IdScreen
- Added MicIcon, ChevronDownIcon, VideoOffIcon to Icons.jsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: BUG-046/047 修复 3D 模式底图和音频中断问题

BUG-046: 修复 Ctrl+9 局部 3D 模式底图不显示
- 根因: render() 函数在 2D 模式下不保存 lastFrame_
- 修复: 在 render() 中无论 2D/3D 模式都保存 lastFrame_
- 效果: 切换到 ONLY_LEFT 模式时正确显示底图

BUG-047: 修复音频播放突然中断问题
- 根因: EOF 收到但队列仍有数据包时调用 avcodec_flush_buffers 导致无限循环
- 修复: 不 flush 解码器，等待队列自然处理完剩余数据包
- 效果: 音频播放稳定，不再突然中断

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent FMA contraction in non-FMA scalar expected computation

Use volatile to force separate mul+add in compute_scalar_expected_nofma,
preventing MSVC from contracting to FMA. This ensures the scalar reference
matches SSE intrinsics (_mm_mul_pd + _mm_add_pd) exactly over 10K iterations.

commit.message:
fix(ux): v3.7.60 — 더블클릭 2개 인스턴스 버그 근본 원인 수정

[버그 1] run_vibe.bat PID 체크가 실제로 작동 안 하던 문제
  - Windows BAT if 블록 내 %OLD_PID%는 블록 진입 시 확장되어 항상 빈 문자열
  - setlocal enabledelayedexpansion + !OLD_PID! 로 변경 → 정상 작동

[버그 2] _MAX_INSTANCES=4 로 소켓 락이 4개 슬롯 허용
  - 이전 인스턴스 소켓이 아직 살아있어도 다음 슬롯으로 두 번째 실행 허용됨
  - _MAX_INSTANCES=1 로 변경 → 단일 인스턴스 보장

[추가] 종료 시 _lock_sock 명시적 close
  - os._exit(0) 전에 락 소켓을 직접 닫아 다음 실행에서 포트 즉시 재사용 가능

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix active status to use account status, not recruitment status

ContractorAccountStatusHistory is the source of truth for active/
inactive in Qwylo, not CurrentRecruitmentStatusId. A contractor
can be 'Hired' but deactivated at the account level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add macOS support technical discussion

Document dyld interpose mechanism, two-level namespace behavior,
errno differences, SIP constraints, and implementation decisions
including why #[link_section] was chosen over global_asm!.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add macOS usage instructions and SIP restriction notes

Add macOS to supported platforms, document DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES usage,
and warn about SIP preventing injection into /usr/bin/ system binaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-detect macOS/Linux target and library name in Makefile

Use uname -s to detect the platform and set TARGET, LIB_NAME,
and CARGO_CMD accordingly. macOS defaults to native cargo build
with .dylib output; Linux defaults to cargo zigbuild with .so output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add macOS support with dyld interpose mechanism

Linux uses LD_PRELOAD with symbol overriding, but macOS requires
__DATA,__interpose section for dyld interposition. Key changes:

- Add set_errno() helper with cfg(target_os) for __errno_location vs __error
- Add call_original_connect() with cfg(target_os) for dlsym(RTLD_NEXT) vs
  direct libc::connect (dyld resolves to original in interpose context)
- Register interpose table via #[link_section = "__DATA,__interpose"]
- Extract connect_hook() as shared entry point for both platforms
- Compile original.rs only on Linux (macOS uses libc::connect directly)

perf: fast-path serde skip, batch entity queries, eliminate N+1 contradiction checks

- parse_mem_row: skip serde_json for common defaults (privacy="Private",
  tags=[], metadata={}, links=[]) — avoids ~40% of JSON parsing per row
- New query_facts_by_entities: single SQL query with OR conditions
  replaces N individual query_facts_by_entity calls in multi-hop expansion
- Ingest contradiction check: preload all relevant facts once via
  query_facts_by_entities, then filter in-memory instead of N SQL queries
  per extracted fact

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enhance error detection credibility + comprehensive CI test system

CPU: Prime verification DB (32 primes + 24 composites), FMA 10K iterations,
4-seed multi-round verification, EXTREME random sampling, CACHE/LARGE_DATA
checksum verification.

RAM: Cache flush (clflush/mfence), complementary checkerboard pattern,
bandwidth verification, bit-level XOR analysis, mlock warning.

GPU: VRAM 7 patterns (March C-, Butterfly, Random added), 3-frame artifact
sampling, FMA compute cross-verification.

Storage: FUA flags (O_SYNC/WRITE_THROUGH), per-type error statistics.

PSU: Power-error correlation analysis, 4-level health status
(HEALTHY/MARGINAL/UNSTABLE/FAILED).

CI: 46→51 tests, JSON output validation (N1-N5), GUI assertions 13→25+,
CLI-UI consistency checks, assertion results file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v0.5.0 — subagent memory layer

Indexes Claude Code subagent transcripts (subagents/agent-*.jsonl) into
SQLite with parent-child session linking. Closes the knowledge loss gap
where 95-98% of subagent reasoning was discarded at 40:1 compression.

New:
- AgentSession model + AgentSessionStore (FTS5 over agent transcripts)
- AgentSessionScanner: walks ~/.claude/projects/**/subagents/agent-*.jsonl,
  extracts parentSessionId/agentId/slug from isSidechain JSONL fields
- V3__agent_sessions.sql: agent_sessions table + FTS5 virtual table +
  INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers + indexes
- McpServer: kcp_memory_subagent_search + kcp_memory_session_tree tools
  (8 tools total, up from 6); AgentSessionScanner wired into run()
- 10 new tests (AgentSessionScannerTest + AgentSessionStoreTest): 23 total

Docs:
- README: new Subagent memory section, updated MCP table, releases table
- llms.txt + knowledge.yaml: bumped to v0.5.0

Run kcp-memory scan to retroactively index all existing subagent transcripts
and link them to their parent sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Lower FT eval threshold 500→150 trades, retire all PF<0.5 models

- Lowering MIN_FT_TRADES_EVAL from 500 to 150 trades
- Retiring 10 existing FT models with PF<0.5 and 150+ trades
- Adding clear_ft_backlog.py utility for immediate cleanup

Models retired (all short direction):
- 343-442 trades, PF 0.19-0.49, PnL -0.66% to -6.18%

This frees up tournament slots for fresh challengers and
prevents weak models from accumulating in forward test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add hierarchical Agent Teams with builder UI, execution engine, and MCP tools

Data model:
- AgentTeam (id, userId, name, goal, status ACTIVE|PAUSED)
- AgentTeamMember (role HEAD|COORDINATOR|EXECUTOR|REPORTER, level, hierarchy via reportsToMemberId)
- AgentTeamTask (title, description, status PENDING→IN_PROGRESS→COMPLETED, priority, assignedTo)

Team Builder UI:
- /dashboard/teams — list page with create modal, template selector (Sales/Support/Content)
- /dashboard/teams/[id] — detail page with 4 tabs:
  - Hierarchy: React Flow tree visualization with role-colored nodes
  - Tasks: Kanban board (Pending/In Progress/Completed/Failed)
  - Activity: chronological event feed
  - Analytics: stats cards (total tasks, completed, active members)

Execution engine:
- POST /api/teams/[id]/execute — sends goal to Claude, decomposes into subtasks
  assigned to team members based on role/responsibilities
- HEAD receives goal → delegates to COORDINATORs → COORDINATORs delegate to EXECUTORs
- REPORTERs collect and summarize results

3 pre-built templates (Sales Team, Support Team, Content Team) with full
agent hierarchies, system prompts, and role assignments.

API routes: /api/teams (CRUD), /api/teams/[id]/members, /api/teams/[id]/tasks,
/api/teams/[id]/execute, /api/teams/templates

5 MCP tools: kiln_list_teams, kiln_create_team, kiln_add_team_member,
kiln_assign_task, kiln_get_team_status

Sidebar: added "Agent Teams" nav item between Orchestration and Integrations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
